subject: GRB 250717A: Fermi GBM Final Real-time Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely LONG GRB

At 03:48:11 UT on 17 Jul 2025, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM) triggered and located GRB 250717A (trigger 774416896.059134 / 250717158).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data, is RA = 22.9, Dec = 3.5 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 01h 31m, 3d 30'), with a statistical uncertainty of 2.9 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 60.0 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2025/bn250717158/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn250717158.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2025/bn250717158/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn250717158.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2025/bn250717158/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn250717158.gif
